comment: Speak scenario specs (PLAN2 §3.4). 12 everyday-France scenarios in priority order; gen_scenarios.py generates 3 branching-dialogue variants per scenario from the variant_angles. icon = SF Symbol name.

scenarios.title: Café order
scenarios.setting_blurb: A Paris café. Get a table, order, handle the follow-ups, and pay — without switching to English.
scenarios.npc_role: waiter at a busy Parisian café
scenarios.user_goal: sit down, order a drink (and maybe food), handle the waiter's follow-up questions, get the bill and pay
beats: greeting and getting a table or ordering at the counter
a waiter follow-up question (with milk? terrace or inside? anything to eat?)
asking for or receiving the bill
branch_ideas: waiter asks: anything to eat with that?
they're out of what you ordered — pick something else
pay by card or in cash
variant_angles: Morning, relaxed: a coffee and a croissant on the terrace, small talk about the weather at the end
Afternoon: your first drink choice is unavailable, you pick an alternative and ask for the wifi code
Rush hour at the counter: quick order to take away, the card machine acts up, you sort out payment

scenarios.title: Restaurant — full meal
scenarios.setting_blurb: Dinner at a bistro: menu questions, ordering courses, drinks, dessert, and the bill.
scenarios.user_goal: get seated, ask about a dish, order a starter and main with drinks, decide on dessert or coffee, get the bill
beats: arriving with or without a reservation, getting a table
asking what a dish is or what the waiter recommends
ordering starter and main course plus drinks
branch_ideas: the waiter recommends two dishes — choose one
how do you want the meat cooked
dessert or just a coffee
variant_angles: Evening with a reservation: full three-course dinner, asking what a dish on the menu actually is
Weekday lunch: the plat du jour and the formule, asking what today's special comes with
Dinner with a friend: your first choice of main is finished, and you ask to split the bill at the end

scenarios.title: Boulangerie & market
scenarios.setting_blurb: Buy bread, pastries, and produce — quantities, prices, and the ritual 'et avec ceci ?'.
scenarios.user_goal: greet, ask for items with quantities, respond to 'anything else?', ask the price, pay with small cash
branch_ideas: well-baked or lighter baguette (bien cuite ou pas trop)
the vendor suggests something extra — accept or decline
exact change or a big bill
variant_angles: Morning boulangerie run: a baguette and croissants, they ask how baked you want the bread
Sunday market fruit-and-veg stand: quantities in kilos, the vendor offers a taste, you pick what looks good
Cheese stall at the market: you ask for a recommendation for tonight, quantity by weight, a big bill to break

scenarios.title: Clothing shop
scenarios.setting_blurb: Browse, ask for your size, try things on, and pay — or politely escape the sales pitch.
scenarios.npc_role: sales assistant in a clothing shop
scenarios.user_goal: browse without pressure, ask for a size, try something on, react to how it fits, decide and pay
beats: responding to 'je peux vous aider ?' (just looking, or asking for help)
asking for an item in your size
reacting to fit (too small, too big, just right)
deciding to take it or not, and paying
branch_ideas: it fits or you need another size
take it now or think about it
pay by card or cash
variant_angles: Looking for jeans: the first pair is too small, you ask for the size up and take them
Buying a jacket as a gift for a friend: you ask about the return policy and whether they can gift-wrap it
The soldes: you ask if the sale price is really the final price and hesitate between two items

scenarios.title: Métro & train
scenarios.setting_blurb: Tickets, directions, platforms — and what to do when your line is down.
scenarios.npc_role: ticket-window agent (or a helpful fellow passenger)
scenarios.user_goal: buy the right ticket, find your line or platform, confirm the direction, handle a disruption
beats: asking for a ticket, carnet, or pass
asking which line or platform for your destination
confirming the direction or the last stop
branch_ideas: single ticket or a carnet
the direct line is down — alternative route or bus
first class or second class on the train
variant_angles: Métro ticket window: you buy a carnet and ask how to get to Montmartre, which line and direction
Gare de Lyon: a ticket to Lyon this afternoon, which platform, and whether you can get a window seat
Your métro line is partly closed: a fellow passenger explains the workaround with one change

scenarios.title: Asking directions
scenarios.setting_blurb: Stop a stranger, follow two-step directions, and confirm you got it right.
scenarios.user_goal: politely stop someone, ask where something is, follow multi-step directions, confirm your understanding, thank them
beats: confirming by repeating back the key steps
branch_ideas: on foot or is it too far — take the bus?
they offer a landmark shortcut vs. street-by-street directions
they're not sure — ask someone else or check together
variant_angles: You need a pharmacy: it's a five-minute walk with two turns, you repeat the directions back to check
Looking for the nearest métro: it's far, the passerby suggests the bus instead and tells you which stop
Your phone is dead and you're looking for a small street: the passerby knows the area and walks you through it via a landmark

scenarios.title: Hotel & Airbnb
scenarios.setting_blurb: Check in, get the practical info, and get problems fixed without drama.
scenarios.user_goal: check in under your name, get key and practical info (wifi, breakfast, floor), report a problem later, get it resolved
beats: the practical rundown (key, floor, wifi, breakfast times)
reporting an issue (heating, hot water, noise)
branch_ideas: breakfast included or added now
the issue: fixed tonight or room change
late checkout — possible or not
variant_angles: Smooth hotel check-in: reservation found, you ask about breakfast times and the wifi code, plus late checkout
Airbnb: the host shows you around, explains the quirks, and later you message about no hot water
Hotel: they can't find your reservation at first, then the heating doesn't work and you ask to change rooms

scenarios.title: Pharmacy
scenarios.setting_blurb: Describe what hurts, answer the pharmacist's questions, and leave with the right thing.
scenarios.user_goal: describe a minor ailment, answer 'since when' and follow-up questions, understand the dosage, pay
beats: answering 'depuis quand ?' and one more follow-up
branch_ideas: tablets or effervescent
do you have a prescription — no, something over the counter
the stronger option or the gentle one
variant_angles: A cold and a sore throat since yesterday: the pharmacist asks about fever and recommends two things
A headache plus blisters from walking all day: two problems, two products, dosage for each
An upset stomach: they ask what you ate, you ask whether to take it with food and how often

scenarios.title: Phone calls & bookings
scenarios.setting_blurb: The hardest mode: no faces, no gestures. Book a table, an appointment, a callback.
scenarios.npc_role: person answering the phone (restaurant host, salon receptionist, doctor's secretary)
scenarios.user_goal: open the call, state what you want, negotiate a date and time, spell your name, confirm and close
beats: opening the call and stating your purpose
proposing a date, time, and party size
handling 'nothing available then' with an alternative
confirming the details and closing the call
branch_ideas: 8pm is full — earlier, later, or another day
terrace or inside / with which stylist / morning or evening slot
leave a phone number for confirmation
variant_angles: Calling a restaurant for a table for two tonight: 8pm is full, you take 9pm and spell your name
Booking a haircut: negotiating a Thursday slot, choosing morning or after work
Calling the doctor's office: describing roughly why, the only slot is Friday, you leave your number

scenarios.title: Small talk
scenarios.setting_blurb: A stranger starts chatting. Keep up, react naturally, and exit gracefully.
scenarios.npc_role: friendly stranger (in a queue, a park, an elevator)
scenarios.user_goal: handle an opener, answer where you're from and what you do, react naturally to what they say, exit politely
beats: the opener (weather, the wait, the dog)
where are you from / how long have you been here
what do you do — and asking them back
reacting naturally to something they tell you
branch_ideas: admit you're learning French or press on like a local
ask them a question back or let them carry it
exchange pleasantries and go, or linger
variant_angles: The post-office queue: mutual complaining about the wait, they ask where your accent is from
The elevator neighbor: introductions, how long you've been in the building, an invitation to the building apéro
A park bench: their dog breaks the ice, weather, then weekend plans — you mention yours

scenarios.title: Bar & apéro
scenarios.setting_blurb: Order a round, toast, meet a friend-of-a-friend, and hold your own in fast chat.
scenarios.npc_role: bartender, then a friend-of-a-friend at the table
scenarios.user_goal: order a round, toast, introduce yourself to someone new, answer the classic questions, decide on one more or heading home
beats: ordering a round at the counter
the classic questions (what do you do, how do you know X)
one more round or calling it a night
branch_ideas: pression or a cocktail
same again or something different
stay for one more or head home
variant_angles: At the counter: ordering two beers, chatting with the bartender about the neighborhood, opening a tab
A friend's birthday drinks: you're introduced to Camille, the how-do-you-know-each-other exchange
After-work apéro: splitting the next round, then deciding between one more and the last métro

scenarios.title: Problems & complaints
scenarios.setting_blurb: Wrong order, broken purchase, lost bag: state the problem, stay polite, get it fixed.
scenarios.npc_role: shop assistant, waiter, or lost-and-found agent
scenarios.user_goal: state the problem calmly with the key details, listen to the options, insist politely if needed, reach a resolution
beats: insisting politely when the first answer is weak
branch_ideas: refund or exchange
accept the offer or ask for the manager
the item was found vs. leave your contact details
variant_angles: Returning a sweater that shrank in the wash: receipt in hand, choosing refund or exchange
At a restaurant: the wrong dish arrives, and then the bill has an extra item on it
Lost-and-found at the station: you left a bag on the train and describe it — contents, color, which train
